pattern_recognition: Tree Traversal, Binary Search Tree Properties; signals include the need to find a common ancestor based on BST properties where left children are smaller and right children are larger.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Traverse the entire tree and store the paths from the root to both nodes, then compare the paths to find the last common node.
approaches.algorithm: 1. Find the path from root to p and root to q. 2. Compare the paths to find the last common node.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Utilize the properties of a BST to directly find the LCA without storing paths, leveraging the fact that all nodes to the left are smaller and all nodes to the right are larger.
approaches.algorithm: 1. Start from the root. 2. If both p and q are smaller than root, move to the left child. 3. If both are larger, move to the right child. 4. If one is on the left and the other is on the right, the current root is the LCA.

dry_run: Given a BST with values [6, 2, 8, 0, 4, 7, 9, null, null, 3, 5], and we want to find the LCA of 2 and 8. Starting at root (6), since 2 < 6 and 8 > 6, we find that 6 is the LCA.
edge_cases: Empty tree (null root), single node tree, both nodes being the same, one node being the ancestor of the other, large trees with depth greater than typical recursion limits.
common_pitfalls: 1. Confusing the LCA with the lowest node in the tree. 2. Not considering the case where one node is the ancestor of the other. 3. Failing to handle duplicate values in the BST.
